deliverables: Parti diagram and massing model
3D conceptual model with massing respecting 7m HOA height limit
Material direction board (contemporary palette; natural stone and stucco per HOA covenant)
Color and texture direction
Preliminary space arrangement aligned with 337 sqm area program
Preliminary concept submitted to HOA design review committee (Residencial Vista del Mar) for early feedback
scope_phases.milestone_trigger: Phase 1 complete when client provides written approval of conceptual direction AND HOA early feedback has been received
scope_phases.revision_assumptions: 2 rounds of client revision included. Each additional round billed at MXN 8,000/day.

deliverables: Floor plans all levels (1:100 scale)
Elevations all four facades (1:100) — natural stone and stucco facade per HOA covenant
Building sections minimum 2 (1:100)
Roof plan — no flat roofs over 2 floors per HOA covenant
Site plan with property boundaries, setbacks, access, parking
Interior design criteria document
Window and door schedule
Preliminary structural coordination sketch (Oficio Taller coordinates with structural engineer)
HOA design review submission package (floor plans + elevations in format required by Residencial Vista del Mar)

Covenant compliance checklist confirming: setbacks, maximum height (7m), facade materials (natural stone/stucco), parking count, prohibited uses, landscaping requirements, contemporary style compliance
Written HOA approval document (obtained by Oficio Taller on behalf of client)
scope_phases.milestone_trigger: Phase 2 complete when client provides written approval of architectural design AND written HOA approval from Residencial Vista del Mar is received
scope_phases.revision_assumptions: 2 rounds of client revision included (including HOA compliance). HOA-driven revisions beyond 2 rounds billed as change orders at MXN 10,000/day.
scope_phases.notes: Municipal permit application cannot proceed until HOA written approval is received. Collaborators required: structural engineer, electrical engineer, plumbing/hydraulic engineer (all coordinated by Oficio Taller; client contracts engineers directly).

deliverables: Structural engineering drawings integrated with architectural set
Electrical single-line diagram and panel schedules
Lighting layout plan with fixture specifications
Plumbing and hydraulic layout
Irrigation plan (included per template default for residential)
Solar systems coordination (included — client confirmed solar in program)
Smart home / AV systems coordination (included — client confirmed smart home in program)
Foundation plan and details
scope_phases.revision_assumptions: 1 round of structural coordination revision included. Changes driven by client program additions billed additionally.
scope_phases.notes: Solar and smart home included in scope per client's confirmed special_features. Landscape architecture excluded by default (client responsibility for independent landscape architect).

deliverables: Fully coordinated construction drawing set (architectural + structural + MEP)
Detail sheets: wall sections, stairs, roof details, openings
Finish schedule (all surfaces, all rooms)
Door and window schedule with specifications
Built-in furniture drawings (kitchen, closets, built-in shelving)
Technical specifications document
Final HOA-compliant drawing set bearing HOA approval stamp or reference number from Residencial Vista del Mar
scope_phases.revision_assumptions: 1 round of coordination revision included. Client-initiated changes after executive plan approval require a change order.
scope_phases.notes: All engineers from Phase 3 must deliver coordinated final drawings.

deliverables: Bid package preparation (executive plans + spec document sent to minimum 2 contractors)
Bid comparison matrix (contractor name, total, key line items, timeline, notes)
Recommendation with rationale
Development contractor pre-approval verification (confirm with client whether Residencial Vista del Mar maintains a contractor pre-approval list before Phase 5 begins)
Local contractor cost validation (bid prices reviewed against current Los Cabos construction cost benchmarks)
scope_phases.notes: Minimum 2 bids required. If only 1 contractor responds, matter is escalated to client before proceeding.

deliverables: Building permit application package (architectural plans + structural calculations + forms)
Submission to Municipio de Los Cabos, BCS
Tracking and correction management until permit issued
Developer internal authorization verification (Oficio Taller verifies requirement with client during Phase 2)
scope_phases.notes: Permit timeline is authority-dependent. HOA approval from Phase 2 is a prerequisite.

payment_schedule.name: Concept Design Approved
payment_schedule.trigger_event: Client written approval of Phase 1 (and HOA early feedback received)

payment_schedule.name: Construction Documents Delivered
payment_schedule.trigger_event: Executive plans package + HOA-approved drawing set delivered

payment_schedule.name: Building Permit Obtained
payment_schedule.trigger_event: Municipal permit document delivered to client

payment_schedule.name: Construction Administration Final
payment_schedule.trigger_event: Final site visit report delivered (if supervision in scope); otherwise triggers at construction start

payment_schedule_notes: Payment amounts derived from seed data payment_schedule where available. M1 matches seed data (USD 27,200 = 40%). M2 and M3 recalculated from template percentages applied to USD 68,000 total fee. Seed data payment_schedule used as primary reference for M1.

responsibilities_matrix.deliverable: HOA design review submission — Residencial Vista del Mar

exclusions: Structural, electrical, plumbing, and civil engineering fees — client contracts engineers directly; Oficio Taller coordinates only
Permit fees, municipal taxes, or government charges
Contractor selection or construction contracts — Oficio Taller recommends; client decides
Furniture, art, décor, or movable equipment not shown in built-in furniture drawings
Topographic survey — required; client responsibility to provide
Hydrologic or soil study (site conditions show no hydrology concerns; if municipality requires one, client responsibility)
Legal review of property title or easements
Construction cost guarantees — estimates are preliminary; final cost determined by contractor bids
HOA fees, development assessment fees, or Residencial Vista del Mar design review committee fees
Legal challenges to HOA decisions or covenant disputes — refer to attorney
Design changes required by HOA feedback beyond 2 included revision rounds — billed as change orders
Landscape architecture — excluded by default; included only if client requests as an additional service
Construction administration / supervisión arquitectónica — not included in this scope unless added by change order

revision_assumptions.phase_1: 2 rounds included. Additional rounds billed at MXN 8,000/day.
revision_assumptions.phase_2: 2 rounds included (including HOA compliance). HOA-driven revisions beyond 2 rounds billed as change orders at MXN 10,000/day.
revision_assumptions.phase_3: 1 round included. Changes driven by client program additions billed additionally.
revision_assumptions.phase_4: 1 round included. Client-initiated changes after Phase 4 approval require a change order.
